When working with DataFrames, we might need to change the column or index labels.
Luckily for us, Pandas offers different ways to perform this task.
First, let’s import Pandas
import pandas as pdLet’s create a Dictionary with students’ names and their results in a few subjects. We will use a code instead of the subject name.
# Create a Dictionary called test_datatest_data = {'Student' : ['John', 'Mark', 'Lisa', 'Alice', 'Paul', 'Jack'],
'MAT2732': [86, 67, 94, 78, 70, 82],
'PHY8816': [92, 77, 90, 85, 77, 78],
'BIO0012': [84, 95, 92, 87, 80, 85]}
From test_data, create a DataFrame called test_results that also includes an index with labels from One to Six. Name the index ID.
# Create test_results test_results = pd.DataFrame(test_data, index = ['One', 'Two', 'Tree',
'Four', 'Five', 'Six'])
test_results.index.name = 'ID'
print(test_results)
Student MAT2732 PHY8816 BIO0012
ID
One John 86 92 84
Two Mark 67 77 95
Tree Lisa 94 90 92
Four Alice 78 85 87…
